meta.name: Heartburst
meta.description: A love / like / favorite moment: a heart swells with a double-beat (lub-dub) then bursts into a flurry of small hearts that fly outward, arc, and fade — warm OKLCH reds/pinks/corals.

intensity.label: Intensity
intensity.help: Beat strength + number of burst hearts + glow.

whimsy.label: Whimsy
whimsy.help: Soft photoreal gloss heart (0) to flat cel sticker heart (1).

seed.label: Seed
seed.help: Null = unique warm palette + burst per fire; pin to reproduce.

tempo.note: Datafied per-frame logic (the web/Swift/Kotlin heartburst tempo code, delta-0): a lub-dub double beat (two cos^k pulse lobes at life 0.10 / 0.21, the second scaled by doubleBeat), then the burst easing out over the rest of life. amp tracks the beats and the burst flare; presence snaps in / holds / fades the panel; beat is the thump amplitude; burst the flight progress; flash the warm light spike on each thump + the release.

heart.note: Parametric heart curve drawn in heartburst-renderer drawHeartburstPanel (hero + flurry).

pass.note: Per-PASS uniforms: the halftone cell is authored in CSS-ish px and scaled to device px on the host (uDotSize = dotSize * dpr).

panel.note: The Canvas2D/CoreGraphics/android.graphics panel (hero heart + burst flurry, R=fill G=ink B=burst) the host redraws every frame; the draw itself is the per-platform panel-draw seam (web hooks / Heartburst Panel files).

webgl2.note: Hybrid: a Canvas2D panel (hero heart + little hearts, vector heart curves) is uploaded as a texture; the shader adds bloom, gloss, halftone blush, beat flash and casts warm light + shadow.

binding.note: CROSS-PLATFORM uniform-binding contract. Which render.params are NOT shader uniforms, the seed-keyed scatter field, the per-frame/host extras, and the texture samplers — one source of truth for the web u<Name> list, the Swift struct + packer, and the MSL struct. SHIPS in the portable .dope (the runtime derives its uniform bindings from it); the toolchain consumes it too for the Metal struct codegen. Only `x-build`, `slug` and `kind` stay toolchain-only.

extras.name: dotSize
extras.note: halftone cell size in device px (dpr-scaled)
